class.name: Rope
class.description: The class renders "rope" and models the physics using verlet integration. It supports attaching nodes to arbitrary positions along the rope, as well as anchoring the rope to other objects in a scene through [class RopeAnchor]. [b]Force Feedback:[/b] When anchored to [RigidBody3D] nodes, the rope automatically applies tension forces back to the attached bodies, enabling realistic physics interactions. Use [member tension_force_scale] to control force strength and [member max_tension_force] to prevent instability. [b]Anchor Array:[/b] The rope supports any number of mid-rope anchors via the [member anchor_count] array. Each anchor has an offset, an optional node path, a behavior, and a friction value. Use [member anchor_distribution] to control how offsets are interpreted. [b]Subclassing:[/b] Override [method _update_anchors] to push dynamic anchor data from code, and call [method _notify_anchors_changed] to trigger a rebuild. Override [method _update_attachments] and [method _get_attachment_local_transform] similarly for attachments.

method.name: _get_attachment_local_transform
method.description: Override to return the local-space transform for the attachment at [param attach_idx]. Called by the rope when positioning attachment nodes along the rope. Call [method _notify_attachments_changed] after updating attachment data to trigger a rebuild.

method.name: _notify_anchors_changed
method.description: Marks the anchor list as dirty and schedules a rebuild. Subclasses that override [method _update_anchors] should call this after they have updated their anchor data. If [method _update_anchors] is not overridden, this also calls [method Object.notify_property_list_changed] so the inspector reflects any changes.

method.name: _notify_attachments_changed
method.description: Marks the attachment list as dirty and schedules a rebuild. Subclasses that override [method _update_attachments] should call this after they have updated their attachment data.

method.name: _update_anchors
method.description: Override to push dynamic anchor data into the rope. This is called during the physics update when the anchor list is dirty. Use the [code]set_anchor_*[/code] methods to configure individual anchors inside this override.

method.name: _update_attachments
method.description: Override to push dynamic attachment data into the rope. Called during the physics update when the attachment list is dirty. Override [method _get_attachment_local_transform] to provide a transform per attachment index.

method.name: clear_anchors
method.description: Removes all mid-rope anchors. Equivalent to setting [member anchor_count] to [code]0[/code].

method.name: get_all_links
method.description:
Returns all link transforms. The link transform is oriented midway between two adjacent particles and is used to position collision shapes and chain-link meshes.
There will be N-1 links for N simulation particles.

method.name: get_all_rope_frames
method.description: Returns all render frame transforms. Frame tangents and normals are aligned along the rope; more frames are generated at higher [member appearance] LOD settings.

method.name: get_anchor_abs_offset
method.description: Returns the computed absolute distance in meters from the start of the rope for anchor [param idx], as resolved by [member anchor_distribution].

method.name: get_anchor_behavior
method.description: Returns the behavior of anchor [param idx]. See [RopeAnchor] for behavior values ([code]ANCHORED[/code], [code]GUIDED[/code], [code]SLIDING[/code], [code]TOWING[/code]).

method.name: get_anchor_count
method.description: Returns the number of mid-rope anchors. See also [member anchor_count].

method.name: get_anchor_friction
method.description: Returns the friction coefficient of anchor [param idx]. [code]0.0[/code] means free sliding; [code]1.0[/code] means fully stuck. Only relevant for [code]GUIDED[/code] and [code]SLIDING[/code] behaviors.

method.name: get_anchor_from
method.description: Returns which end of the rope the offset for anchor [param idx] is measured from. Returns [constant Start] or [constant End].

method.name: get_anchor_nodepath
method.description: Returns the [NodePath] assigned to anchor [param idx]. The rope will follow the referenced node's transform. If the node is a [RopeAnchor], its behavior and friction are also applied automatically.

method.name: get_anchor_particle_count
method.description: Returns the number of simulation particles in the segment owned by anchor [param anchor_idx] (between this anchor and the next). Useful for iterating particle positions within a rope segment.

method.name: get_anchor_particle_position
method.description: Returns the world-space position of particle [param particle_idx] in the segment owned by anchor [param anchor_idx].

method.name: get_anchor_transform
method.description: Returns the world-space transform of anchor [param idx].

method.name: get_baked_mesh
method.description: Returns the current mesh of the rope. Changes every physics frame.

method.name: get_current_rope_length
method.description: Returns the current length of the rope in meters. May differ from [member rope_length] when the rope is stretched.

method.name: get_end_offset
method.description: Returns the UV end offset from the [member appearance] resource. Offsets the texture along the rope from the end.

method.name: get_link
method.description: Returns the link transform at [param index], or [constant Transform3D.IDENTITY] if out of bounds.

method.name: get_link_count
method.description: Returns the current number of links. Will be N-1 where N is the number of simulation particles.

method.name: get_particle_count_for_length
method.description: Returns the number of simulation particles in the rope, determined by [member rope_length] and [member particles_per_meter].

method.name: get_particle_positions
method.description: Returns the current world-space positions of all rope simulation particles.

method.name: get_rope_frame
method.description: Returns the render frame transform at [param index], or [constant Transform3D.IDENTITY] if out of bounds.

method.name: get_rope_frame_count
method.description: Returns the current number of render frames. More frames are generated at higher LOD settings.

method.name: get_rope_lod
method.description: Returns the number of Catmull-Rom spline points generated per simulation particle. Read from the [member appearance] resource.

method.name: get_rope_sides
method.description: Returns the number of sides in the rope tube mesh cross-section. Read from the [member appearance] resource.

method.name: get_rope_twist
method.description: Returns the V-coordinate multiplier for texture UV coordinates along the rope length. Read from the [member appearance] resource.

method.name: get_start_offset
method.description: Returns the UV start offset from the [member appearance] resource. Offsets the texture along the rope from the start.

method.name: set_anchor_behavior
method.description: Sets the behavior of anchor [param idx]. See [RopeAnchor] for valid behavior values ([code]ANCHORED[/code], [code]GUIDED[/code], [code]SLIDING[/code], [code]TOWING[/code]).

method.name: set_anchor_count
method.description: Sets the number of mid-rope anchors. Anchors beyond the new count are discarded; new anchors are initialized with default values. See also [member anchor_count].

method.name: set_anchor_friction
method.description: Sets the friction coefficient of anchor [param idx]. Range [code]0.0[/code] (free sliding) to [code]1.0[/code] (fully stuck). Only affects [code]GUIDED[/code] and [code]SLIDING[/code] behaviors.

method.name: set_anchor_from
method.description: Sets which end of the rope the offset for anchor [param idx] is measured from. Pass [constant Start] or [constant End].

method.name: set_anchor_nodepath
method.description: Sets the [NodePath] for anchor [param idx]. The rope will follow the referenced node's world transform. If the node is a [RopeAnchor], its behavior and friction are applied automatically.

method.name: set_anchor_offset
method.description: Sets the offset of anchor [param idx] along the rope. Interpretation depends on [member anchor_distribution]: [br]- [constant ABSOLUTE]: meters from the start (or end if [code]anchor_from[/code] is [constant End]) [br]- [constant RELATIVE]: offset relative to the previous anchor, can be negative [br]- [constant UNIFORM]: ignored; anchors are spaced evenly [br]- [constant SCALAR]: [code][0..1][/code] mapping start to end [br]- [constant REAL]: auto-calculated from the initial node transform

method.name: set_anchor_transform
method.description: Sets the world-space transform of anchor [param idx] directly. Useful when no node path is assigned.

members.member: Number of mid-rope anchor points. Use the [code]anchor_*[/code] methods or the Inspector array to configure each anchor's offset, node path, behavior, and friction.

Controls how anchor offsets are interpreted. One of: [br]- [constant ABSOLUTE]: offset in meters from the start (or end when [code]from[/code] is [constant End]) [br]- [constant RELATIVE]: offset relative to the previous anchor; can be negative [br]- [constant UNIFORM]: anchors are spaced evenly; offsets are ignored [br]- [constant SCALAR]: offset in [code][0..1][/code] mapping the full rope length [br]- [constant REAL]: offset auto-calculated from each anchor node's initial world position
A [RopeAppearance] resource containing properties that control how the rope is rendered, including material, number of sides, LOD level, and UV twist.
When [code]true[/code], applies buoyancy forces to rope particles that are submerged in the [member liquid_area]. Requires a valid [member liquid_area] to have any effect.
When [code]true[/code], applies velocity damping to the rope simulation each frame. Use [member damping_factor] to control the strength.
When [code]true[/code], applies the [member gravity] vector to all rope particles each physics frame. Use [member gravity_scale] to adjust magnitude.
When [code]true[/code], applies wind forces to the rope simulation. Direction and strength are set by [member wind] and [member wind_scale]. Noise variation can be added with [member wind_noise].
Multiplier for the upward buoyancy force applied to submerged particles. A value of [code]1.0[/code] causes the rope to float at the surface. Requires [member apply_buoyancy] to be enabled.
The physics layers this rope's collision shapes occupy. Ignored when the Jolt Physics engine is active (collision shapes are disabled for performance).
The physics layers this rope's collision shapes scan for colliders. Ignored when the Jolt Physics engine is active.
Velocity damping applied per physics frame when [member apply_damping] is enabled. Higher values cause the rope to lose energy faster and come to rest sooner.
When [code]true[/code], draws debug overlays for anchor positions, particle positions, and tension forces using [member debug_color].
Color used for debug visualization when [member debug] is enabled.

Global friction coefficient for sliding anchors. At [code]0.0[/code] the rope slides freely. At [code]1.0[/code] the rope sticks. Individual anchors can override this via [method set_anchor_friction].
Gravity vector applied to the rope simulation each physics frame when [member apply_gravity] is enabled.
Scale factor applied to [member gravity] before adding it to rope particles. Use values less than [code]1.0[/code] for lighter rope behavior.

Controls which end of the rope particles are added to or removed from when [member rope_length] changes. [constant Start] grows and shrinks from the start; [constant End] grows and shrinks from the end.

When [code]true[/code], small random offsets are added to particle positions when the rope is first built. This breaks symmetry and helps the simulation settle into a natural curve instead of a straight line.
Reference to a [LiquidArea] node used to sample the water surface height for buoyancy calculations. Requires [member apply_buoyancy] to be enabled.

Maximum force magnitude (in Newtons) that can be applied to attached [RigidBody3D] objects per physics frame. This clamp prevents explosive forces during extreme rope stretching or fast body motion, ensuring simulation stability. [b]Usage Guidelines:[/b] - For light objects (1-10 kg): 100-500 N is usually sufficient - For heavy objects (100+ kg): Increase to 5000+ N for stronger coupling - If bodies fly apart unexpectedly, lower this value - If rope feels too weak, increase this value or [member tension_force_scale]

Number of simulation particles generated per meter of rope length. Higher values produce more accurate simulation and smoother curves at the cost of performance. Affects particle count, link count, and render frame count.

Virtual time in seconds to simulate before the first frame is rendered. Runs [code]preprocess_time * simulation_rate[/code] physics ticks as fast as possible at startup, so the rope appears in its resting pose immediately.
Length of the rope in meters. Segment length will be [code]rope_length / particle_count[/code]. Changing this triggers a rope rebuild.
Diameter of the rendered rope mesh in meters. Also controls the radius of collision shapes generated along the rope.
When [code]false[/code], the rope physics simulation is paused. The rope mesh continues to be rendered at its last simulated position.
Rate in Hz at which the rope simulation runs. Higher values improve accuracy at the cost of performance. The simulation accumulates time and runs multiple steps per rendered frame when necessary.

Stiffness factor controlling how much the rope resists stretching. Values around [code]0.8[/code]–[code]1.2[/code] produce realistic rope behavior. Higher values may cause instability; increase [member stiffness_iterations] to compensate.
Number of constraint-solving passes per simulation step. More iterations improve stiffness accuracy and stability at high [member stiffness] values, at the cost of performance.
Drag coefficient applied to rope particles while submerged in a [member liquid_area]. High values cause the rope to slow rapidly under water. Requires [member apply_buoyancy] to be enabled.

Multiplier for constraint forces applied to attached [RigidBody3D] objects. Controls how strongly the rope pulls on connected bodies. Force calculation: [code]applied_force = constraint_correction * stiffness * body_mass * tension_force_scale[/code] [b]Usage Guidelines:[/b] - [code]0.0[/code]: Disables force feedback entirely (rope doesn't affect bodies) - [code]0.1-0.5[/code]: Weak coupling, soft/stretchy rope behavior - [code]1.0[/code]: Default, balanced rope-body interaction - [code]2.0-5.0[/code]: Strong coupling, rigid rope-like behavior - [code]5.0+[/code]: Very stiff, may cause instability with light objects [b]Note:[/b] Forces are automatically clamped by [member max_tension_force] to prevent instability.
Wind direction and base magnitude applied to the rope when [member apply_wind] is enabled. Scale the effect with [member wind_scale].

Optional [FastNoiseLite] resource used to add temporal variation to the wind force. When set, wind strength is modulated by the noise value sampled at each particle's position and the current simulation time.
Scale factor applied to the [member wind] vector before adding it to the rope simulation.

constants.constant: Offsets and growth are measured from the start of the rope.
Offsets and growth are measured from the end of the rope.
Anchor offsets are absolute distances in meters from the start (or end) of the rope.
Anchor offsets are relative distances in meters from the previous anchor. Negative values move backwards along the rope.
Anchors are distributed uniformly along the rope. Offset values are ignored.
Anchor offsets are scalar values in [code][0..1][/code], where [code]0.0[/code] is the start and [code]1.0[/code] is the end.
Anchor offsets are automatically calculated from each anchor node's initial world-space position relative to the rope.
